title: Executive Summary
content: WPC closes 2025-12-31 with $1.72B of revenue (8.4% YoY) and $466M of net income (1.2% YoY). The net margin of 27.2% is the clearest guide to how much of reported revenue reached the bottom line. Revenue and earnings improved together, indicating that the growth story carried through to reported profitability. Operating cash flow was $1.28B (-30.0% YoY).

title: Key Metrics Dashboard
content:
*Snapshot period: FY2025. Prior period: FY2024.*

The dashboard uses the same row order across reports, and values the filing does not disclose remain explicitly marked rather than being hidden.
*Asset Turnover: Asset turnover uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Book Value Per Share: Book Value Per Share uses period-end shares outstanding.*
*Earnings Per Share (Diluted): Earnings Per Share (Diluted) uses diluted weighted-average shares from the income statement.*
*Return on Assets: ROA uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Return on Equity: ROE uses average equity for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*

title: Trend Analysis
content:
Across the annual periods shown here, operating cash flow provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $1.28B and a compound annual growth rate of 19.4%. The multi-year progression is steady enough to suggest an improving operating profile.

The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

title: Risk Factors
content: No single balance-sheet or earnings issue dominates the reporting period used in this note. Even so, the absence of a hard red flag does not eliminate the need to monitor margin durability, cash conversion, and balance-sheet flexibility.

*Appendix figures use FY2025 and FY2024.*
*Appendix rows follow the standard statement line set used throughout the report.*
*Cash-family labels follow the filing directly, and the cash-flow ending balance remains separate unless the filing presents the same concept on the balance sheet.*

*Asset Turnover: Asset turnover uses average assets for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Book Value Per Share: Book Value Per Share uses period-end shares outstanding.*

*Return on Equity: ROE uses average equity for each annual period only when the adjacent annual balance is available.*
*Primary filing anchor: 10-K filed 2026-02-11.*

title: Analyst Notes
content:
WPC's profile does not reduce to a single standout strength or weakness; the more relevant question is how the income statement, cash flow statement, and balance sheet interact.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-31, with the prior period set at 2024-12-31. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
